output: Completed batch 4 task T6 in `tests/test_admin_payments.py`. The admin-payments test doubles now support the shared status-message flow end-to-end: `FakeChannel` returns editable/reply-capable/fetchable message objects that record suppress flags and reply metadata, and `FakeIntentDB` now carries a minimal Supabase stub plus a `reject_status_message_id` toggle so the real `DatabaseHandler.update_admin_payment_intent` retry path can be exercised. I updated the stale assertions for the new in-place status-message behavior and added the six requested coverage tests for admin-chat result-card suppression, non-admin-chat preservation, the missing-column retry contract, full lifecycle reuse, post-approval status-message edits, and cleanup preservation. Verification: `python -m py_compile tests/test_admin_payments.py` passed and `pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py -q` passed with `67 passed`.

task_updates.executor_notes: Extended `tests/test_admin_payments.py` with richer Discord message/channel doubles and the planned retry-capable DB stub. `FakeChannel` now returns stored message objects with `edit`, `reply`, and `fetch_message` support plus recorded `suppress_embeds` / reply kwargs (`tests/test_admin_payments.py:71-133`). `FakeIntentDB` now exposes a minimal Supabase stub with `reject_status_message_id`, `supabase_failures`, and `supabase_calls` so `DatabaseHandler.update_admin_payment_intent` can be exercised directly (`:336-411`). Updated the stale admin-chat payment assertions for the shared status-message lifecycle (`:1557-1612`, `:1686-1725`, `:2255-2297`, `:2331-2376`) and added the six requested coverage tests for admin-chat worker suppression, non-admin-chat notification preservation, missing-column retry/FLAG-002, full status-message reuse, post-approval edits, and cleanup preservation (`:2109-2178`, `:2514-2623`, `:2627-2700`). Verification: `python -m py_compile tests/test_admin_payments.py` passed and `pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py -q` passed (`67 passed`).

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. `tests/test_admin_payments.py` now contains the `FakeChannel` send/edit/reply/fetch_message extensions (`:71-133`), the `FakeIntentDB` Supabase-backed retry stub with `reject_status_message_id` (`:336-411`), the updated existing assertions for the shared status-message flow (`:1557-1612`, `:1686-1725`, `:2255-2297`, `:2331-2376`), and all six requested new tests (`:2109-2178`, `:2514-2623`, `:2627-2700`). The DB retry test explicitly asserts both the `status_message_id` retry omission and the unrelated-column `None` return contract, and the lifecycle test asserts exactly one base status message, three edits, and one reply.
